query: Fast, local-first, Open-Source dictation built for you alternative

results.title: Show HN: Whispering – Open-source, local-first dictation you can trust
results.content: ```  # Stop recording kill $(cat /tmp/dictate.pid) # Transcribe whisper-cli --language en --model $HOME/.local/share/whisper/ggml-large-v3-turbo-q8_0.bin --no-prints --no-timestamps /tmp/dictate.mp3 | tr -d '\n' | sed 's/^//;s/$//'  ```  I keep these in a dictate.sh script and bind to press/release on a single key. A programmable keyboard helps here. I use  to turn the transcription into keystrokes. I've also tried ydotool and wtype, but they seem to swallow keystrokes.  ```  bindsym XF86Launch5 exec dictate.sh start bindsym --release XF86Launch5 exec echo "type $(dictate.sh stop)" | dotoolc  ```  This gives a very functional push-to-talk setup. I'm very impressed with . Transcription quality with large-v3-turbo-q8\_0 is excellent IMO and a Vulkan build is very fast on my 6600XT. It [...] | |  |  | Show HN: Whispering – Open-source, local-first dictation you can trust (github.com/epicenter-so) | | 591 points by braden-w 10 months ago | hide | past | favorite | 152 comments | | Hey HN! Braden here, creator of Whispering, an open-source speech-to-text app. I really like dictation. For years, I relied on transcription tools that were almost good, but they were all closed-source. Even a lot of them that claimed to be “local” or “on-device” were still black boxes that left me wondering where my audio really went.  So I built Whispering. It’s open-source, local-first, and most importantly, transparent with your data. Your data is stored locally on your device, and your audio goes directly from your machine to a local provider (Whisper C++, Speaches, etc.) or your chosen cloud [...] and the best solution was to build our own. | | | |  |  | fwip 10 months ago  | parent | prev | next (javascript:void(0))   Doesn't an accurate transcription make it easier to reach understanding? | | | |  |  | hereme888 10 months ago  | prev | next (javascript:void(0))   Earlier today I discovered Vibe:  Local, using WhisperX. I'm hoping to find and try a local-first version of an nvidia/canary like (like ) since it's almost twice as fast as Whisper with even lower word-error-rate | | | |  |  | icelancer 10 months ago  | parent | next (javascript:void(0))   Been using WhisperX myself for years. results.title: Top 6 Open Source Transcription Software Tools in 2025 - Amical
results.content:
## Why Amical is the Best Local and Open Source Alternative to Transcription Apps in 2025

Many open-source tools like Whisper or Vosk are excellent transcription models but require technical setup to use. Amical bridges this gap by packaging these models into a user-friendly desktop app for Mac and Windows, enabling instant dictation and meeting transcription without coding or configuration.

### Built for Privacy-First Users

Amical is designed for individuals and organizations that value data security and confidentiality. Unlike cloud-only solutions, it allows you to run AI models entirely on your local machine. This ensures that sensitive conversations, such as board meetings, legal discussions, or personal notes, never leave your device.

### Open Source & Fully Transparent [...] 1. ### Amical

Amical is an open-source transcription and note-taking platform powered by generative AI, designed for users who want faster, smarter, and more context-aware voice input. Unlike traditional transcription software, Amical not only converts speech into text with exceptional accuracy but also adapts to the context of your application, formatting text appropriately for professional emails, casual social posts, or project updates.

Unlike Whisper, Vosk, or Kaldi, which are transcription models, Amical is a desktop application for Mac and Windows that integrates these models and provides dictation and meeting transcription features at the push of a button. [...] | Feature | Amical | Vosk | Kaldi | Whisper | DeepSpeech | WhisperX |
 ---  ---  --- 
| Open Source | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Platform | Windows, Mac | Windows, Linux, Android, iOS, Raspberry Pi | Linux (Windows with WSL) | Browser (Self-host), Linux, Windows | Windows, Linux, Raspberry Pi | Linux, Windows |
| Dictation app | Yes | No | No | No | No | No |
| Push to talk | Yes | No | No | No | No | No |
| Offline Support | Yes (Local Models) | Yes | Yes | Yes (Local Deployment) | Yes | Partial (Needs GPU for best performance) |
| Cloud Support | Yes (Optional, BYO API) | No | No | No | No | Yes (Optional) |
| Bring Your Own API Key | Planned | No | No | No | No | Yes (Hugging Face for diarization) |

results.title: I Tried the Open Source ElevenLabs Alternative (Voicebox)
results.content:
# I Tried the Open Source ElevenLabs Alternative (Voicebox)
## Better Stack
163000 subscribers
1873 likes

### Description
61985 views
Posted: 17 Jun 2026
Voicebox is a free open-source local AI voice studio for developers that brings voice cloning, text-to-speech, system-wide dictation, agent voice output, MCP integration, Stories editing, and a local REST API into one polished desktop app. 

In this video, I test Voicebox and show why devs are paying attention to it as a local-first alternative to cloud AI voice tools like ElevenLabs, especially if you care about privacy and unlimited generation.

🔗 Relevant Links
Voicebox Repo - 
Voicebox - 

❤️ More about us
Radically better observability stack: 
Written tutorials: 
Example projects: [...] from my machine and I cloned my own voice. There was no browser tab. I didn't need API keys. But here's the part that feels like this is a real workflow. The system-wide dictation. I could hit a global hot key and I could say whatever I'm thinking in the moment. If you like finding coding tools and tricks like this, check out our channel. Now it lands directly inside my editor. So I mean, that was pretty useful for notes, comments, or anything like that. But all these little moments where talking is actually faster than typing, that's huge. This is not only for you talking to the computer, your agents could actually talk back now. Cloud code, cursor, or your own local agent can trigger speech through Voicebox instead, instead of only just dumping it into your terminal. We're already [...] Voice Box like a tool. Instead of your agent only replying with text, it now speaks back to you. But do you want to hear yourself speak back to you? I I don't know. Maybe change the voice for that. But imagine your coding agent saying, "Build failed. Three test modules broke the off module." That sounds not real until you realize how many times a day you're already getting feedback from your tools. Voice Box just gives those updates an actual voice. So, why did I like this one so much compared to others? Well, okay. Privacy and cost. Honestly, those are the really big wins, at least for me. Those are easy wins. For voice samples, audio, internal content, or anything really sensitive, local first is what we want. It's great. Then is the agent integration, which I didn't put into the full

results.title: OpenWhispr | Open Source Voice to Text & Dictation App
results.content:
Keisuke

Keisuke

@\_AlwaysAI

Translated from Japanese

The best voice input solution I've found recently. I was using Aqua Voice before, but this is OSS with the same features and feel — plus you can choose between API-based pay-as-you-go or downloading a local ASR model!

DJ

DJ

@DuaneJRich

Reading is faster than listening, and speaking is faster than writing. Makes me think the highest throughput person-to-AI mode will be voice to text and reading / evaluating outputs. I'm trying this with OpenWhispr for voice to text.

MV

MV

@MaximVovshin

OpenWhispr is a really good open source alternative for WisprFlow!

Adrian Nutiu

Adrian Nutiu

@AdrianNutiu

Whisper Base local is near instant in OpenWhispr, and I found it better than Parakeet. Parakeet is near instant, too.

Anagh

Anagh [...] Dictation, AI cleanup, offline mode, and 100+ languages.

### Your machine. Your models.

Run local speech-to-text models or bring your own API keys. All free.

Whisper Tiny

Fastest75 MB

Whisper Base

Fast142 MB

Whisper Small

Balanced466 MB

Whisper Medium

Accurate1.5 GB

Whisper Turbo

Best1.6 GB

### Give instructions by voice.

Say things like 'clean this up' or 'draft an email to Mike' and it does exactly that.

You said

"hey OpenWhispr, clean up this email — I need to tell mike that the meeting is moved to thursday at 3 and also remind him about the deck"

Output

Hi Mike,  
  
Quick heads up — our meeting has been moved to Thursday at 3 PM. Also, just a reminder to have the deck ready.  
  
Thanks!

### It learns your words. [...] ### Zero data retention

When you use cloud processing, your audio passes through for transcription and is never stored. No recordings, no logs, no copies.

### Everything stays on your device

Your transcription history lives in a local database on your machine. We can't access it even if we wanted to.

### Local AI that stays local

Whisper and Parakeet run directly on your hardware. No audio is sent anywhere. No internet needed.

### We never train on your data

We will never use your transcriptions to train models or improve our services without your explicit consent.

### Fully open source

Our code is public on GitHub for anyone to inspect, audit, and verify. You don't have to take our word for any of this.

## What you get

Dictation, AI cleanup, offline mode, and 100+ languages.

results.title: Best Offline Speech Recognition Software 2026: 6 Tools Ranked
results.content:
### whisper.cpp — Open-Source Power (Technical Users)

whisper.cpp is the open-source C/C++ implementation of OpenAI’s Whisper model that has become the foundation for many commercial offline dictation tools. With 46,900+ stars on GitHub and support for Metal, CUDA, and Vulkan GPU acceleration, it delivers impressive accuracy at zero cost.

Key considerations:

whisper.cpp is ideal for developers and technical users who want maximum control. If you prefer a ready-to-use application built on the same technology, tools like Weesper Neon Flow wrap whisper.cpp in a polished interface with professional features. For Linux users specifically, whisper.cpp is just one of several strong options — see our full roundup of open-source voice dictation tools for Linux for a dedicated comparison.
